additionalTime: 10 minutes patty seasoning rest and sauce rest
author: Robert Carr
description: One thick A5 Wagyu burger with a toasted potato or brioche bun, glossy soy-pickle onions, a little burger sauce, pickles, lettuce, and a skillet cook that builds a crust before gently finishing the center.
equipment: Cast-iron skillet or stainless steel skillet
Lid or small sheet pan for covering the skillet

groceryIngredients.display: A5 Wagyu blended beef burger patty
groceryIngredients.original: A5 Wagyu blended beef burger patty

groceryIngredients.item: butter lettuce or red leaf lettuce

ingredients: 1 A5 Wagyu blended beef burger patty, about 1 inch thick and 4 inches wide, kept cold until cooking
1 potato bun or brioche burger bun, ideally about 4 inches wide
1/4 small yellow onion, with 3 tablespoons sliced paper-thin for the pan onions
1 butter lettuce or red leaf lettuce leaf, patted dry
Small pinch garlic powder, about 1/16 teaspoon
1/4 teaspoon Diamond Crystal kosher salt or 1/8 teaspoon Morton kosher salt, divided
1/8 teaspoon black pepper, freshly ground, divided
Small pinch MSG, about 1/16 teaspoon, optional
instructions: Put the 1 A5 Wagyu blended beef patty on a paper towel-lined plate and pat the top, bottom, and sides very dry.
If the patty has puffed into a dome, press only the center lightly with your thumb to make a shallow dimple about 1 inch wide. Do not flatten or smash the patty.
Mix 1/4 teaspoon Diamond Crystal kosher salt, 1/8 teaspoon black pepper, and the small pinch MSG if using in a small bowl.
Season the top, bottom, and sides of the patty with almost all of the salt, pepper, and MSG mixture, saving only a tiny pinch of the mixture for the onions.
Refrigerate the seasoned patty uncovered for 10 minutes while you make the sauce and prepare the toppings.

Make the burger sauce by stirring together 1 tablespoon mayonnaise, 1/2 teaspoon ketchup, 1/2 teaspoon Dijon mustard, 1/4 teaspoon pickle brine, 1/4 teaspoon Worcestershire sauce, and the small pinch garlic powder.
Refrigerate the sauce for 10 minutes while the patty rests so the mustard, pickle brine, and Worcestershire season the mayonnaise.
Slice 3 tablespoons yellow onion paper-thin, then separate the slices with your fingers so they cook quickly.
Pat 1 butter lettuce or red leaf lettuce leaf dry and set out 3 to 4 dill pickle chips so the burger can be assembled as soon as the patty finishes.
Melt 1 teaspoon unsalted butter in a cast-iron or stainless steel skillet over medium heat.
Add the 3 tablespoons sliced yellow onion and the reserved tiny pinch of the salt, pepper, and MSG mixture.
Cook the onions for 3 to 4 minutes, stirring often, until softened and lightly browned at the edges.
Stir 1/2 teaspoon low-sodium soy sauce, the remaining 1/2 teaspoon pickle brine, 1/8 teaspoon granulated sugar, and 1 teaspoon water into the onions.
Cook for 30 to 60 seconds, stirring constantly, until the liquid is gone and the onions look glossy, then transfer the onions to a small plate.
Wipe the skillet clean if any onion sugar stuck to the pan, then return the skillet to medium heat.
Spread the remaining 1 teaspoon unsalted butter over the cut sides of the potato bun or brioche bun.
Toast the bun cut-side down for 1 to 2 minutes, until the cut sides are golden and crisp at the edges, then transfer the bun to a plate.
Increase the skillet to medium-high heat for 1 minute. The pan should be hot enough to brown the patty steadily but not so hot that the rendered Wagyu fat smokes hard.
Pat the cold seasoned patty dry again if surface moisture appears.
Place the patty in the hot dry skillet and cook without pressing for 2 to 3 minutes, until the bottom has a deep brown crust.
Flip the patty with a thin metal spatula and cook the second side uncovered for 2 minutes.

Reduce the heat to medium-low, cover the skillet with a lid or small sheet pan, and cook for 2 to 4 minutes more, until the center of the ground beef patty reaches 150 degrees F to 155 degrees F when checked from the side with an instant-read thermometer.
Place 1 thin slice American cheese on the patty, cover again, and cook for 30 to 60 seconds more, until the cheese melts and the center reaches 160 degrees F.
Transfer the cheese-topped patty to a clean plate and rest it for 2 minutes while you sauce the bun.
Spread about 2 teaspoons burger sauce on the toasted bottom bun.
Add the dry butter lettuce or red leaf lettuce leaf and 3 to 4 dill pickle chips to the sauced bottom bun.
Place the rested cheese-topped Wagyu patty on the pickles.
Spoon all of the glossy soy-pickle onions over the patty.
Spread the remaining burger sauce on the toasted top bun.
Close the burger and serve immediately while the bun is crisp and the patty is hot.
notes: This makes exactly 1 burger with 1 thick patty.
A 1-inch-thick, 4-inch-wide patty needs a normal 4-inch burger bun. A slider bun would make the burger awkward and bread-light in the wrong places.
For this 30-minute version, buy the bun. The beef, onions, sauce, and toast matter more than making bread.

The flavor comes from a dry beef surface, enough exterior seasoning, a short cold rest, a hot dry pan, quick soy-pickle onions, melted American cheese, pickles, and a restrained sauce that does not bury the Wagyu.
Do not smash this patty. It is thick enough to cook as a juicy steakhouse-style burger, and pressing hard can squeeze out too much rendered fat.
Do not add oil to the skillet before searing the patty. Wagyu-style ground beef renders quickly, and extra oil can make the burger greasy instead of rich.
The covered finish matters for a 1-inch-thick patty. It lets the center come up to temperature before the crust gets too dark.
For food safety, cook ground beef patties to 160 degrees F. Start checking early because a rich, thick patty can move from juicy to overdone quickly near the end.

If the patty is browning too fast before the center reaches 150 degrees F, lower the heat and keep the skillet covered. If it is cooking through before browning, uncover it and raise the heat slightly.
Keep the sauce light. The burger should taste like rich beef first, then toast, onions, cheese, pickles, and sauce.
Serve with fries, chips, or a small salad. The burger is rich enough that simple sides are best.
personalNotes: This burger was so damn good and easy to make. Probably the best I can remember eating. The pan will smoke a lot, so make sure it won't set off a fire alarm. I used Dave's Killer Bread Burger Buns Done Right for the buns which was so much easier than making the buns at home with the burger. The burger will also be very juicy.
title: A5 Wagyu Burger
